comments.text: This page should probably record the following (located using GPT 5.4 Thinking): 1. Lovász proved the finite analogue: if $H$ is a hypergraph on a finite set $X$, all edges have size at least $2$, and there are no edges $E,F$ with $|E\cap F|=1$, then $X$ has a $2$-partition such that every edge meets both classes. 2. If the index set is countable, the answer is yes, and the intersection condition is unnecessary. This is Bernstein's Lemma: every countable system of infinite sets has Property B. In fact, the name “Property B” was given in honour of Bernstein’s result. 3. If there is a fixed $n<\omega$ such that $|A_i\cap A_j|\leq n$ for all $i\neq j$, then the answer is yes. This is Miller’s theorem on strongly almost disjoint families. In fact, Komjáth later proved the stronger statement that every such family of countable sets is essentially disjoint; after deleting finitely many points from each member, the remaining sets can be made pairwise disjoint. This immediately gives a splitting in which each $A_i$ meets both colour classes infinitely often. 4. Pairwise finite intersections alone are not enough. Hajnal, Juhász and Shelah record that there is an $\omega$-almost disjoint subfamily of $[\omega]^\omega$ which is not $2$-colourable, originally due to Miller. Thus the extra hypothesis $|A_i\cap A_j|\neq1$ is needed.
